patch.value:
## Problem

Merged-head Release Draft run `29893136465` succeeded but emitted five deprecation annotations. The configuration still uses legacy `categories[*].labels` and top-level `exclude-labels`, which Release Drafter will remove.

## Source

Release Drafter PR 1558 is the authoritative migration guide. It moves category predicates under `when`, replaces top-level excludes with a `type: pre-exclude` category, and represents version resolution through category `semver-increment` plus `type: version-resolver` entries.

## Scope

Migrate `.github/release-drafter.yml` without changing generated category grouping or version-bump semantics. Verify repository workflows, YAML parsing, local release gates, PR checks, bot review, and a merged-head Release Draft run with zero annotations.
Migrate Release Drafter category configuration before legacy removal
Remove merged-head Release Drafter deprecation annotations while preserving changelog grouping and version resolution.

Release Drafter configuration uses current category classification syntax; feature/fix/docs/maintenance grouping, skip-changelog exclusion, and major/minor/patch resolution semantics are preserved; merged-head Release Draft has zero annotations; all local and GitHub gates pass
Merged-head annotations captured; all-status duplicate search completed; upstream Release Drafter PR 1558 migration guide reviewed.
Keep release automation warning-free and production-ready.
Eliminate deprecated Release Drafter configuration before removal.
Prevents future release-draft breakage and keeps exact-head checks annotation-free.
Release notes and semantic version suggestions retain behavior on supported configuration.
Release Draft completes successfully with zero annotations.
The exact merged head exposes five deprecation warnings and upstream states the fields will be removed.
GitHub Actions release-drafter/release-drafter@v7 on merged master 191672e
Inspect annotations for Release Draft run 29893136465 job 88837476936.
Release Draft succeeds with five deprecation warnings for legacy category labels and exclude-labels.
Future Release Drafter upgrades can break draft generation after deprecated fields are removed.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Exact merged-head check annotations captured: four category label warnings and one top-level exclude-labels warning.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Migrate version-resolver semantics too because upstream PR 1558 unifies all change classification, even though the current run did not annotate that block.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A successful GitHub check can still carry actionable deprecation annotations; exact-head annotation inspection is required.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Migrated four changelog categories to when.labels with preserved semver increments, replaced skip-changelog with pre-exclude, added explicit breaking-change major resolution, and retained default patch resolution. Full local release gates pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Authoritative migration source: https://github.com/release-drafter/release-drafter/pull/1558. The current syntax uses the unified category classification pipeline introduced there.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Default patch resolution is represented by a final version-resolver category without a when predicate; specific breaking-change major resolution precedes it, while changelog categories carry minor/patch increments directly.

patch.value: Configuration parses to seven current categories with no legacy label/exclude keys; full release gate passes. Merged-head zero-annotation proof will be captured after PR merge.
Release Drafter accepts supported configuration without deprecation annotations and preserves release-note/version behavior.
Replaced deprecated category labels, top-level exclusion, and legacy version resolver with when predicates, pre-exclude, semver increments, and version-resolver categories.
Migrated Release Drafter classification syntax while preserving grouping and version resolution.
